title: How to compare whether two images with same tag are consistent?
body:
I once used 'skopeo copy' to pull a certain image from Dockerhub to the local, such as xxx: latest. After a period of time, I want to compare whether my local xxx: latest image is consistent with the xxx: latest image on the current dockerhub.

I noticed that the images on Dockerhub all have a digest, but after pulling it through Skopeo, this information is no longer available.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out. That’s a somewhat multifaceted question, e.g. is an uncompressed image “consistent” with the original compressed one? The answer is situation-specific, e.g. it’s fine for `podman pull` but it’s not for a ~transparent registry mirror.

> from Dockerhub to the local,

To the local _what_ ? What transport?

> but after pulling it through Skopeo, this information is no longer available.

`skopeo inspect` always shows a digest value. Whether that digest at destination is reliably the same as at the source depends on options, and the transports involved – and a digest comparison might not be _exactly_ what you are after.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Please reopen if there is more to discuss.
